---
title: "San Diego Taco Awards"
format: html
---

This report reads `data/menus.csv` with a **relative path**. Quarto runs it
with the working directory set to the folder the `.qmd` file lives in — so
rendering works no matter where you call it from:

```bash
quarto render report.qmd
```

```{python}
import csv
from pathlib import Path

with open(Path("data") / "menus.csv") as f:
    rows = list(csv.DictReader(f))
for r in rows:
    r["price"] = float(r["price"])
    r["rating"] = float(r["rating"])

print(f"{len(rows)} menu items from {len({r['shop'] for r in rows})} shops")
```

## Top 10 tacos

```{python}
top = sorted(rows, key=lambda r: -r["rating"])[:10]
print(f"{'shop':<20} {'neighborhood':<14} {'taco':<12} {'price':>6} {'rating':>6}")
print("-" * 62)
for r in top:
    print(f"{r['shop']:<20} {r['neighborhood']:<14} {r['taco']:<12} "
          f"{r['price']:>6.2f} {r['rating']:>6.1f}")
```

## Average rating by neighborhood

```{python}
hoods = {}
for r in rows:
    hoods.setdefault(r["neighborhood"], []).append(r["rating"])
for hood, ratings in sorted(hoods.items(), key=lambda kv: -sum(kv[1]) / len(kv[1])):
    avg = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
    print(f"{hood:<14} {'*' * round(avg * 4)}  {avg:.2f}")
```

After rendering, open `report.html` in VS Code (right-click -> Open Preview, or
just click it) — this is how a script becomes something you can share.
